/** Every supported Hugeicons Pro style in the Kura icon foundation. */
export const HUGEICONS_PRO_STYLES = [
  "bulk-rounded",
  "duotone-rounded",
  "duotone-standard",
  "solid-rounded",
  "solid-sharp",
  "solid-standard",
  "stroke-rounded",
  "stroke-sharp",
  "stroke-standard",
  "twotone-rounded",
] as const;

export type HugeiconsProStyle = (typeof HUGEICONS_PRO_STYLES)[number];
/** @deprecated Use HugeiconsProStyle. Kept for existing Kura icon consumers. */
export type HugeiconsStyle = HugeiconsProStyle;

/** Chevron geometry stays crisp across compact disclosure and navigation controls. */
export const KURA_CHEVRON_ICON_STYLE: HugeiconsProStyle = "stroke-standard";

type KuraIconVariants = Record<HugeiconsProStyle, IconSvgElement>;

export type KuraIconProps = Omit<
  ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof HugeiconsIcon>,
  "altIcon" | "icon" | "showAlt"
> & {
  /** Selects one of the ten licensed Hugeicons Pro visual styles. */
  iconStyle?: HugeiconsProStyle;
};

export type KuraIconComponent = ForwardRefExoticComponent<
  KuraIconProps & RefAttributes<SVGSVGElement>
>;

function createKuraIcon(
  name: string,
  variants: KuraIconVariants,
  defaultStyle: HugeiconsProStyle = "stroke-rounded",
): KuraIconComponent {
  const Icon = forwardRef<SVGSVGElement, KuraIconProps>(function KuraIcon(
    {
      "aria-hidden": ariaHidden = true,
      "aria-label": ariaLabel,
      iconStyle = defaultStyle,
      size = "1em",
      ...props
    },
    ref,
  ) {
    return (
      <HugeiconsIcon
        ref={ref}
        icon={variants[iconStyle]}
        size={size}
        aria-hidden={ariaLabel ? undefined : ariaHidden}
        aria-label={ariaLabel}
        data-kura-icon={name}
        data-kura-icon-style={iconStyle}
        focusable="false"
        {...props}
      />
    );
  });

  Icon.displayName = `${name}Icon`;
  return Icon;
}

design-system-primitives-accordion.name: Accordion

type.description: Single keeps one section open; multiple allows comparison across sections.

collapsible.description: A single section may be closed when this is enabled.

elements.name: Array
elements.raw: string[]

stories.name: Single Open
stories.snippet: const SingleOpen = () => <ClinicalContextAccordion defaultValue="identity" />;

stories.name: Multiple Open
stories.snippet: const MultipleOpen = () => <WorkflowAccordion />;

stories.name: Disabled Item
stories.snippet:
const DisabledItem = () => (
  <Accordion
    aria-label="Patient record sections"
    type="single"
    collapsible
    defaultValue="available"
  >
    <AccordionItem value="available">
      <AccordionTrigger>Visit summary</AccordionTrigger>
      <AccordionContent>The visit summary is available to the current role.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
    <AccordionItem value="restricted" disabled>
      <AccordionTrigger>Restricted clinical notes</AccordionTrigger>
      <AccordionContent>Only clinicians with the required permission can review these notes.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
  </Accordion>
);

ratio.description: Width divided by height. Use a functional media requirement, not a decorative crop.

design-system-primitives-avatar.name: Avatar

size.description: Semantic size; md follows the root density setting for operational lists.

shape.description: Use circle for people and rounded for compact identity marks.

fallbackTone.description: Semantic fallback treatment used when an image is unavailable.

elements.name: union
elements.raw:
| 'neutral'
| 'brand'
| 'success'
| 'warning'
| 'danger'
| 'info'
| 'ai'

ring.description: Optional semantic ring for review, presence, or active identity states.

ringAnimation.description: Pulse is optional and automatically disabled for reduced-motion users.

loading.description: Shows a stable loading overlay while profile data is being resolved.

design-system-primitives-badge.name: Badge

variant.description: Status or metadata meaning; badges are non-interactive by default.

appearance.description: Soft is the default for scan-friendly status labels.

dot.description: Optional status dot; pair it with text rather than relying on color alone.

leading.description: Canonical leading content such as a semantic icon or compact avatar.

trailing.description: Canonical trailing content for metadata or a compact avatar.

as.description: Use only for navigation. Badges must not become button-like actions.

em dash so corrupt data cannot crash the component tree.

design-system-components-alert-dialog.name: AlertDialog

open.description: Controlled open state for workflow-owned confirmation logic.

defaultOpen.description: Initial open state for an uncontrolled dialog.

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet:
const Default = () => (
  <AlertDialog>
    <AlertDialogTrigger asChild>
      <Button variant="outline">Remove draft</Button>
    </AlertDialogTrigger>
    <AlertDialogContent>
      <AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogTitle>Remove this draft?</AlertDialogTitle>
        <AlertDialogDescription>
          This removes the unsent draft from your workspace. This action cannot be undone.
        </AlertDialogDescription>
      </AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogFooter>
        <AlertDialogCancel>Keep draft</AlertDialogCancel>
        <AlertDialogAction variant="destructive">Remove draft</AlertDialogAction>
      </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
  </AlertDialog>
);

stories.name: Compact Confirmation
stories.snippet:
const CompactConfirmation = () => (
  <AlertDialog defaultOpen>
    <AlertDialogContent size="sm">
      <AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogTitle>Allow session sharing?</AlertDialogTitle>
        <AlertDialogDescription>
          The selected colleague can view this record during the active consultation.
        </AlertDialogDescription>
      </AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogFooter>
        <AlertDialogCancel>Do not allow</AlertDialogCancel>
        <AlertDialogAction>Allow sharing</AlertDialogAction>
      </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
  </AlertDialog>
);

stories.snippet:
const WithMedia = () => (
  <AlertDialog defaultOpen>
    <AlertDialogContent>
      <AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogMedia tone="info"><InformationIcon aria-hidden="true" /></AlertDialogMedia>
        <AlertDialogTitle>Start the record review?</AlertDialogTitle>
        <AlertDialogDescription>
          Opening the review reserves this record for your current session until you finish or release it.
        </AlertDialogDescription>
      </AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogFooter>
        <AlertDialogCancel>Review later</AlertDialogCancel>
        <AlertDialogAction>Start review</AlertDialogAction>
      </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
  </AlertDialog>
);

stories.name: Destructive Confirmation
stories.snippet:
const DestructiveConfirmation = () => (
  <AlertDialog defaultOpen>
    <AlertDialogContent size="sm">
      <AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogMedia tone="danger"><DeleteIcon aria-hidden="true" /></AlertDialogMedia>
        <AlertDialogTitle>Delete this attachment?</AlertDialogTitle>
        <AlertDialogDescription>
          The file will be permanently removed from this draft. The clinical record itself will not change.
        </AlertDialogDescription>
      </AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogFooter>
        <AlertDialogCancel variant="ghost">Cancel</AlertDialogCancel>
        <AlertDialogAction variant="destructive">Delete attachment</AlertDialogAction>
      </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
  </AlertDialog>
);

stories.name: Unsaved Changes
stories.snippet:
const UnsavedChanges = () => (
  <AlertDialog defaultOpen>
    <AlertDialogContent>
      <AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogMedia tone="warning"><WarningIcon aria-hidden="true" /></AlertDialogMedia>
        <AlertDialogTitle>Leave without saving changes?</AlertDialogTitle>
        <AlertDialogDescription>
          Your edits to the review note have not been saved. Leaving now discards those changes.
        </AlertDialogDescription>
      </AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogFooter>
        <AlertDialogCancel>Keep editing</AlertDialogCancel>
        <AlertDialogAction variant="destructive">Discard changes</AlertDialogAction>
      </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
  </AlertDialog>
);

stories.name: Pending Action

  <AlertDialog defaultOpen>
    <AlertDialogContent size="sm">
      <AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogTitle>Remove the draft now?</AlertDialogTitle>
        <AlertDialogDescription>
          The action is in progress. Keep this dialog open until the operation finishes.
        </AlertDialogDescription>

      <AlertDialogFooter>
        <AlertDialogCancel disabled>Keep draft</AlertDialogCancel>

      </AlertDialogHeader>

        <AlertDialogCancel>Cancel</AlertDialogCancel>

stories.name: Long Content
stories.snippet:
const LongContent = () => (
  <AlertDialog defaultOpen>
    <AlertDialogContent>
      <AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogTitle>Archive the inactive care-plan draft?</AlertDialogTitle>
        <AlertDialogDescription>
          This draft is no longer visible in the active care-plan workspace. It remains in the audit history with its author and timestamp, but it cannot be edited or reopened as an active plan. Confirm that no current follow-up depends on this draft before archiving it.
        </AlertDialogDescription>
      </AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogFooter>
        <AlertDialogCancel>Keep active</AlertDialogCancel>
        <AlertDialogAction variant="destructive">Archive draft</AlertDialogAction>
      </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
  </AlertDialog>
);

design-system-components-card.name: Card

as.description: Choose semantic structure based on whether the card is a standalone item or a page section.

size.description: Explicit inset size. When omitted, the card follows the root Kura density.

variant.description:
Surface separation. Default (`elevated`) is the flat gray tray that sits
on the white page — contrast only, no border or shadow. `tile` is a white
box with a feather shadow for nesting inside a tray. `outline` is a white
surface with a hairline border — for bordered containers and tables.

dividers.description: Adds section dividers when the boundaries are meaningful to the task.

design-system-components-command.name: Command
design-system-components-command.description:
A keyboard-first collection of authorised destinations and commands.
The owning workflow supplies only records and actions the current actor may use.

reactDocgen.description:
A keyboard-first collection of authorised destinations and commands.
The owning workflow supplies only records and actions the current actor may use.

stories.name: Basic
stories.snippet:
const Basic = () => (
  <CommandDialog defaultOpen title="Search Kura" description="Search destinations authorised for this clinic workspace.">
    <Command label="Global Kura search" loop>
      <CommandInput placeholder="Search patient, booking, or action" />
      <CommandList>
        <CommandEmpty>No authorised destination matches this search.</CommandEmpty>
        <CommandGroup heading="Open">
          <CommandItem value="patient-pham-thi-lan" keywords={['patient', 'MRN-01482', 'Phạm Thị Lan']}>
            <MedicalFileIcon aria-hidden="true" />
            Open patient record: Phạm Thị Lan
          </CommandItem>
          <CommandItem value="booking-bk-20491" keywords={['booking', 'BK-20491', 'appointment']}>
            <CalendarIcon aria-hidden="true" />
            Open booking BK-20491
          </CommandItem>
        </CommandGroup>
      </CommandList>
    </Command>
  </CommandDialog>
);

stories.name: Shortcuts
stories.snippet:
const Shortcuts = () => (
  <CommandDialog defaultOpen title="Quick actions" description="Choose a non-destructive action or open its governed workflow.">

      <CommandInput placeholder="Search quick actions" />
      <CommandList>

          <CommandItem value="new-booking" keywords={['booking', 'appointment', 'create']}>

          </CommandItem>
          <CommandItem value="open-patient-search" keywords={['patient', 'find', 'search']}>
            <UserSearchIcon aria-hidden="true" />

            <CommandShortcut><Kbd>⌘P</Kbd></CommandShortcut>
          </CommandItem>
        </CommandGroup>
      </CommandList>
    </Command>
  </CommandDialog>
);

const Actions = () => (
  <DropdownMenu>

design-system-clinical-components-subjec.name: SubjectHeader
design-system-clinical-components-subjec.description:
Identity strip for the top of a workflow: who this is, how the desk refers
to them, and one derived state. Front desk and collection both mount it, so
it names a *subject* rather than a patient — a draw worksheet and a check-in
wizard are about the same person for different reasons.

Adapted from the upstream Kura ui-kit `SubjectHeader` organism (avatar →
title → meta → actions → status, sticky, two-row on mobile). The bordered
meta chips are dropped: the Kura surface direction reserves containers for
regions with their own state, so metadata is dot-separated text.

reactDocgen.description:
Identity strip for the top of a workflow: who this is, how the desk refers
to them, and one derived state. Front desk and collection both mount it, so
it names a *subject* rather than a patient — a draw worksheet and a check-in
wizard are about the same person for different reasons.

Adapted from the upstream Kura ui-kit `SubjectHeader` organism (avatar →
title → meta → actions → status, sticky, two-row on mobile). The bordered
meta chips are dropped: the Kura surface direction reserves containers for
regions with their own state, so metadata is dot-separated text.

tsType.name: signature

properties.description: Rendered with `lang="km"` so Khmer shapes correctly beside the Latin name.
Overrides the derived initials when the record carries its own.
How the desk calls this person today — a queue number or booth PID.
ISO date of birth; rendered with the derived age.
Pre-formatted arrival, e.g. "08:24 · 12 min ago". Never computed here.
Extra facts appended after the derived ones, in the caller's order.

as.description: Heading level, so a page can own its own `h1`.

placeholderTitle.description: Shown while the subject has no name yet.

status.description: One derived state, never an action. The page footer owns the primary action.

actions.description: Trailing controls. Keep to one — this strip is context, not a toolbar.

sticky.description:
Sticks to `--subject-header-offset` (default 0). Set that variable when a
sticky topbar sits above the scroll container.

stories.name: Needs Verification
stories.description: Verification is unfinished — the state is loud, but it is still a fact, not a CTA.

stories.name: With Action
stories.description: A trailing control is allowed, but only one — this strip is context, not a toolbar.

stories.name: Collection Booth
stories.description: The collection booth names the same person by their booth PID.

stories.name: Long Name
stories.description: Long names truncate; the meta line keeps every fact.

design-system-clinical-components-order-.name: OrderCart
design-system-clinical-components-order-.description:
The one clinic order cart (Figma `OrderCart / HBC workflow`): doctor and
receptionist share the shell — selected tests, a collection-and-payment
decision card, totals with clinician earnings, a collected-money
attestation, and one primary action whose label follows the lifecycle.

the next available action. Loading and failure use their dedicated owners.

design-system-patterns-authentication-id.description:
A configured external identity-provider entry point. The owning auth flow
supplies its configuration and handles redirect, state validation, session
recovery, and the work-email fallback; this pattern never performs OAuth.

reactDocgen.description:
A configured external identity-provider entry point. The owning auth flow
supplies its configuration and handles redirect, state validation, session
recovery, and the work-email fallback; this pattern never performs OAuth.

providerName.description: Display name supplied by the configured identity-provider record.

providerBrand.description:
An approved canonical Kura provider mark selected by the auth
configuration. Omit it for a provider without an approved brand mark.

availability.description: Derived from the workspace's identity-provider configuration.

unavailableReason.description: A visible fallback explains why a configured provider cannot be used.

design-system-patterns-otpinput.name: OtpInput
design-system-patterns-otpinput.description:
One-time-code entry. The library renders one accessible input beneath the
segmented visual so native SMS autofill, paste, selection, and screen-reader
behaviour stay equivalent to a standard input.

reactDocgen.description:
One-time-code entry. The library renders one accessible input beneath the
segmented visual so native SMS autofill, paste, selection, and screen-reader
behaviour stay equivalent to a standard input.

groupSize.description: Number of joined slots per visual group. Defaults to 3 for six-digit codes.

value.description: Controlled digit string (may be shorter than `length` while typing).

onComplete.description: Fires once when every digit is filled.

label.description: Visible field label. When omitted, the input receives a generic accessible name.

accessibleLabel.description: Accessible name for label-free compositions. Ignored when `label` is present.

error.description: Error text; also flags the digits invalid.

fullWidth.description: Stretches the visual slot groups to the width of their containing form.

stories.name: Default

design-system-patterns-radio-group.name: RadioGroup

legend.description: Required group label, announced as the radio set's legend.

description.description: Visible context that helps a clinician make the selection safely.

error.description: Specific recovery guidance when no valid choice has been made.

name.description: Shared native form name for every Radio within this group.

defaultValue.description: Initial selected value for uncontrolled use.

onValueChange.description: Called when a selectable option becomes selected.

disabled.description: Disables the complete group while retaining each option's reason nearby.

readOnly.description: Preserves the selected option for review without allowing mutation.

required.description: Applies native required semantics to each option in the group.

orientation.description: Horizontal choices wrap, then stack below the mobile threshold.

layout.description: Grid is a two-column choice layout that stacks at the mobile threshold.

clinic-shell-app-shell.name: AppShell

tsType.name: signature

elements.name: signature
signature
signature

workspaces.description: All workspaces the user belongs to. One workspace hides the switcher chevron.

elements.name: union
elements.raw: 'clinical' | 'front-desk' | 'collection'

availableModes.description: Derived from capabilities — see deriveAvailableModes. One mode hides the switcher.

posture.description: Shell chrome for the current workspace context. Independent from the active mode.

tsType.name: signature

properties.description: Physical booth or desk identifier, e.g. PSC-01.
Station role inside collection mode; one booth measures vitals, another draws.

station.description: Station context for front-desk and collection work.

tsType.name: signature

properties.description: Professional licence verified (KYD). Gates clinical mode, shown in the account menu.

elements.name: signature

properties.description: Group heading. Omit for the primary ungrouped block.

elements.name: signature

properties.description: Item is visible when the actor holds at least one required capability.
Count of items needing attention. Omit zero — render nothing instead of "0".

Roadmap surface that is not usable yet. Rendered muted with a "Soon" tag but
still navigable, so the destination can explain itself instead of a dead
control. Deferred work must never look live.

Collapse the group into one launcher item that opens a menu, keeping the
always-visible rail short. Inside the mobile nav sheet the group still
renders inline — no overlay stacked on an overlay.

nav.description: Navigation for the active mode. Defaults to the mode registry.

permissions.description: Effective capabilities used to remove unauthorized navigation items.

scopeLabel.description: Replaces workspace context when a route is explicitly person-global.

banner.description: Slot above the content for licence lifecycle or outage banners.

headerActions.description: Utility actions shown in the sidebar footer, or in the station/mobile topbar.

contentInset.description: Page gutters by default; full-height workspaces can own their internal spacing.

stories.name: Full Clinic
stories.description: Full clinic: every module enabled, a doctor holding all capabilities.

stories.name: Full Bleed Workspace
stories.description: Edge-to-edge workspace ownership without shell gutters.

stories.name: Solo Doctor
stories.description:
Solo doctor: one mode only → no mode switcher, no switch chrome at all.
The simplest customer never pays complexity tax for the multi-role model.

stories.name: Existing Destinations Only
stories.description: The registry exposes only destinations backed by an app route.

stories.name: Multi Role Nurse
stories.description:
Multi-role nurse: front desk + collection, no clinical (no licence, no
clinical capabilities). The switcher shows exactly the earned modes.

stories.name: Mode Switch
stories.description: Switching modes swaps navigation and lands on the mode's entry destination.

stories.name: Collection Sidebar
stories.description: Collection defaults to the shared navigation shell for mixed-role work.

stories.name: Collection Station
stories.description:
Explicit station posture: no sidebar, station identity + shift in the topbar.
Scan-first booth work is not navigation-first work — the chrome yields.

stories.name: With Licence Banner
stories.description: Licence lifecycle banner in the banner slot — pending review example.

stories.name: With Badge Counts
stories.description: Badge counts on nav items surface work waiting in other destinations.

stories.name: Earnings Person Global Scope
stories.description: Person-owned Earnings appears only with self-ledger access and keeps one active nav item.

stories.name: Earnings Hidden Without Self Access
stories.description: Delegated clinical access never exposes the person-owned Earnings destination.

stories.name: Collapsed Rail
stories.description: Collapsed rail keeps navigation reachable and reverses cleanly under rapid input.

stories.name: Mobile
stories.description: Below 768px the sidebar yields to a left sheet with the same nav.

stories.name: Long Content
stories.description: Long Khmer + English workspace and branch names must truncate, not break layout.

clinic-shell-global-search.description:
The shell-owned ⌘K palette: renders an already permission-filtered registry
through the canonical Command component and owns the global hotkey. Records
resolve through the reception doors only; selecting a consequential action
starts its governed flow — nothing clinical completes inside the palette.

clinic-auth-door.name: Door
clinic-auth-door.description:
The clinic door: one screen where sign-in IS sign-up. Phone OTP is
primary, with email OTP as its alternate. Every path converges on the same
account per identifier. Requesting a code never discloses whether the
identifier already exists; revoked accounts are blocked on every method.

account per identifier. Requesting a code never discloses whether the
identifier already exists; revoked accounts are blocked on every method.

clinic-auth-onboarding-wizard.name: OnboardingWizard
clinic-auth-onboarding-wizard.description:
Product-spec onboarding: self-serve Name → Phone-if-needed → Clinic →
Licence; invitees only supply missing name/phone facts. A verified phone is
the hard gate, Clinic and Licence can be skipped, and cross-account phone
reuse is support-only — never self-merged.

the hard gate, Clinic and Licence can be skipped, and cross-account phone
reuse is support-only — never self-merged.

surface from the account menu.

clinic-clinical-home-signal-tile.name: HomeSignalTile
clinic-clinical-home-signal-tile.description:
The stat-tile composition: one lifecycle axis as a flat grey tray with a
white icon chip, a loud count, its evidence sentence, and the deep link into
the surface that owns the work. The link stretches over the tray, so the
whole tile is the target while the accessible name stays the outcome label.

Reach for it only where peer metrics need a stable comparison boundary. Home
itself renders its signals as rows — a tray needs a functional reason, and
"these counts are related" is not one.

clinic-clinical-care-plan.name: CarePlanCard
clinic-clinical-care-plan.description:
The living plan. Its job on screen is to answer two questions at a glance:
what is this patient's care aiming at, and what is it currently waiting on.

A lab step carries its blocking reason inline rather than a disabled button
with nothing beside it — "waiting on the result" is clinical information,
not a form validation message.

reactDocgen.description:
The living plan. Its job on screen is to answer two questions at a glance:
what is this patient's care aiming at, and what is it currently waiting on.

A lab step carries its blocking reason inline rather than a disabled button
with nothing beside it — "waiting on the result" is clinical information,
not a form validation message.

tsType.name: signature

properties.description: Cadence phrase for the next review, e.g. "3 months".
Bumped once per signed change set, so a plan can be cited by version.
Required whenever the plan is paused.

onCompleteIntervention.description: Mark a step done. Blocked steps never call this.

onRecordException.description: Close an evidence-bearing step that will never produce its evidence.

properties.description: `expired` is the legacy desk contract; lifecycle-native callers use LicenceState.

tsType.name: signature

fxRate.description: Live USD→KHR rate supplied by the clinic config endpoint.

pricingStatus.description: Server pricing availability — the rail blocks collection while not ready.

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet: const Default = () => <WizardPlayground />;
stories.description: New walk-in at step 1 — the gate keeps later steps locked.

stories.name: Duplicate Guard
stories.description: Duplicate detection: same phone + name + DOB flags a strong match and blocks Continue.

stories.name: Paid Edit Choice
stories.description:
Editing a paid cart opens the void-or-supplemental choice as a modal.
The dialog is hidden until requested; the safe path is the dominant action.

stories.name: Full Check In Flow
stories.description: Full happy path through all six steps to check-in, inside the shell.

stories.name: Khqr Payment
stories.description: KHQR path: waiting state, webhook confirmation, cancel escape.

stories.name: KHQR lifecycle · expired, regenerate, manual receipt
stories.description:
The KHQR intent is expiring and cancellable; manual receipt is a confirmed
fallback, never one click. PROTOTYPE: no Bakong provider integration.

stories.name: Split cash + KHQR · two-step collection
stories.description:
Split: cash strictly between zero and the due amount, then a KHQR intent
covers the remainder; the split completes only on Bakong confirmation.
PROTOTYPE: patient payment upstream is cash-only.

stories.name: Mobile
stories.snippet: const Mobile = () => <WizardPlayground />;
stories.description: Mobile: cart rail stacks below the step panel.

stories.name: Prescriber Attribution
stories.snippet: const PrescriberAttribution = () => <WizardPlayground initial={readyWithOrders('attr-1')} />;
stories.description:
Order attribution (ADR-0057): the desk places on behalf of a clinician.
Expired licences cannot be attributed; payment stays blocked until resolved.

stories.name: No Eligible Prescriber
stories.description: Every licence expired → the server would refuse placement (422 NO_ELIGIBLE_PRESCRIBER).

stories.name: Stale Price Reaccept
stories.description:
The server re-derives prices at placement (ORD-21). A stale quote blocks
payment until the desk explicitly accepts the new total.

stories.name: Code Issuance Failed
stories.description:
502 code_issuance_failed: the order EXISTS but no usable code was issued.
The desk sees the needs-attention consequence and a retry — never a dead end.

stories.name: Idempotency Conflict
stories.description: Idempotency conflict (409): an identical placement already succeeded — never retry blindly.

stories.name: Order Composition Blockers
stories.description:
Order composition blockers (truth pack §5.4): panel/analyte overlap,
unsupported test, and the unsupported catalog row disabled with its reason
inline. Payment stays blocked until the cart is clean.

stories.name: Paid With Receipt
stories.description: After payment the receipt artifact replaces the toast-style confirmation.

stories.name: Keyboard · step jumps respect the gate
stories.description: F1–F6 jump steps but never bypass the gate; `?` opens the shortcut map.

stories.name: Promo codes · item → fixed → percent
stories.description:
Promo codes follow the legacy money order — item promo, then fixed, then
percent, each against the running remainder. PROTOTYPE: no upstream engine.

stories.name: Pricing error · collection blocked in the flow
stories.description:
Server pricing unavailable: the rail shows the failure and payment refuses
to collect until pricing returns — the desk never keys an amount by hand.

stories.name: Orders · clinic visit and vitals lines
stories.description:
Visit and vitals lines are orderable desk services — the legacy default
walk-in lines, deliberately added by hand instead of auto-seeded so the
order gate and prescriber attribution stay honest.

stories.name: Mobile dock · sticky next action
stories.description:
The mobile dock begins at order selection, when it has a meaningful next
action. Its CTA resumes at the first not-done step and never bypasses the
gate. The checked-in state renders on CheckInComplete.

clinic-front-desk-check-in-wizard-step-1.name: CheckInWizard
clinic-front-desk-check-in-wizard-step-1.description:
Six-step check-in wizard. The gate engine decides progress; steps never
bypass it. Editing a paid cart routes through the void/supplemental choice.

properties.description: Canonical lifecycle state — display labels derive from this, never freehand.
Branch the code was issued for; reception at another branch cannot redeem it.
Who created the booking — reception on behalf vs the patient themselves.

Capture contract (payment-ms): `paid` is the only real capture state;
`pending` means no capture exists yet. Amount in minor units.

Months since the phone channel was last verified. PROTOTYPE FIELD —
patient-ms stores only the assurance flag, not verification recency.

stories.snippet: const DarkTheme = () => <IdentityPlayground />;

stories.name: Booking Expired
stories.snippet: const BookingExpired = () => <IdentityPlayground />;
stories.description:
Canonical collection-code lifecycle branches. A blocked code is never
silently redeemed: the desk sees the canonical status, why it blocks, and a
recovery door (walk-in with the same record) — never a dead end.

stories.name: Booking Already Redeemed
stories.snippet: const BookingAlreadyRedeemed = () => <IdentityPlayground />;

stories.name: Booking Cancelled Code
stories.snippet: const BookingCancelledCode = () => <IdentityPlayground />;

stories.name: Booking Wrong Branch
stories.snippet: const BookingWrongBranch = () => <IdentityPlayground />;

stories.name: Multiple bookings · pick the one this visit is for
stories.snippet: const MultiBookingCheckIn = () => <IdentityPlayground />;
stories.description:
Check-in binds to ONE booking. A patient with several same-day bookings gets
actionable booking rows (with creator provenance); the walk-in path demotes
to an outline escape. Picking a row advances with that code bound.

stories.name: Trust signals · stale phone verification warns
stories.snippet: const TrustSignalsStaleVerification = () => <IdentityPlayground />;
stories.description:
Verification recency is a trust fact, not decoration: a stale channel warns
before the desk relies on it. PROTOTYPE: patient-ms stores assurance only.

stories.name: Duplicate guard · exact ID needs a supervisor PIN
stories.description:
An exact-ID duplicate cannot be waved through: keeping both records takes a
supervisor PIN and the decision is framed as logged. Lower-tier collisions
keep the one-click acknowledgement.

stories.name: Patient selected · provenance and change patient confirmation
stories.description:
Selection provenance survives: source + time + locked-field count + the
bound booking; changing patients asks first and preserves data until replaced.

provider integration exists upstream.

bypass it. Editing a paid cart routes through the void/supplemental choice.

bypass it. Editing a paid cart routes through the void/supplemental choice.

clinic-front-desk-booking-detail-sheet.name: BookingDetailSheet
clinic-front-desk-booking-detail-sheet.description:
Booking detail inset: one collection code told end-to-end — who it belongs
to, what it orders, whether money moved, and its lifecycle as a timeline.
Blocked codes explain themselves and expose the safe recovery door supplied
by the caller (architecture reference: ReUI `solution-bookings-3`; every
state maps to the canonical collection-code contract).

to, what it orders, whether money moved, and its lifecycle as a timeline.
Blocked codes explain themselves and expose the safe recovery door supplied
by the caller (architecture reference: ReUI `solution-bookings-3`; every
state maps to the canonical collection-code contract).

onOpenDeskQueue.description: Open the desk queue before any walk-in recovery for an already redeemed code.

onConfirmWalkIn.description: Parent-owned confirmation step for a cancelled booking becoming a walk-in.

clinic-front-desk-cart-rail.name: CartRail
clinic-front-desk-cart-rail.description:
Backward-compatible front-desk summary adapter. The shared OrderCart owns
all cart rendering; this adapter keeps the cart in `order-review` while the
Check-In Wizard's Payment step owns tender selection, payment capture, and
check-in confirmation.

clinic-front-desk-desk-queue.name: DeskQueue
clinic-front-desk-desk-queue.description:
Desk arrivals queue (journeys REC-01/02/05). Each row states the
independent lifecycle facts — visit stage, identity assurance, payment —
and offers exactly one next action derived from those axes. Later stages
are owned by phlebotomy and the lab; the desk only observes them.

PROTOTYPE SURFACE: no queue/visit engine exists in kura-platform today.

clinic-collection-tube-labeling.name: TubeLabeling
clinic-collection-tube-labeling.description:
Labelling the tubes after a draw.

The two routes are not equivalent and the interface says so: a printed
Kura sticker carries the order identity a courier and the lab can both
read, while handwriting depends on whoever held the pen. Handwriting stays
available because a clinic without stickers still has to send blood, but it
is the second option and it names what the courier will check.

available because a clinic without stickers still has to send blood, but it
is the second option and it names what the courier will check.

clinic-settings.name: SettingsView
clinic-settings.description:
One canonical surface for low-frequency workspace configuration: identity,
cabinet, team, money, documents, and security. A vertical Tabs rail swaps
dense panels — no hero, no decorative dashboard; status rows and one obvious
action each. The presentation promotes ReUI settings-7's real page, side-tab,
card, and row anatomy into the feature owner while Kura keeps all behavior.

reactDocgen.description:
One canonical surface for low-frequency workspace configuration: identity,
cabinet, team, money, documents, and security. A vertical Tabs rail swaps
dense panels — no hero, no decorative dashboard; status rows and one obvious
action each. The presentation promotes ReUI settings-7's real page, side-tab,
card, and row anatomy into the feature owner while Kura keeps all behavior.

verification.description: Mirrors the verification store; settings never contradicts the gate.

clinic-flows-first-sign-in.name: FirstSignInFlow
clinic-flows-first-sign-in.description:
The complete first-contact journey: Door → (Wizard for new accounts) →
Workspace Gate → the clinic app. A brand-new doctor lands in their own
cabinet (auto-entered — the gate never flashes for a single branch-less
workspace); a returning member picks a workspace and branch instead. The
landing copy reflects source-backed per-member attribution and never turns
licence state into a workspace-wide EXPLORER/PRACTICE mode.

reactDocgen.description:
The complete first-contact journey: Door → (Wizard for new accounts) →
Workspace Gate → the clinic app. A brand-new doctor lands in their own
cabinet (auto-entered — the gate never flashes for a single branch-less
workspace); a returning member picks a workspace and branch instead. The
landing copy reflects source-backed per-member attribution and never turns
licence state into a workspace-wide EXPLORER/PRACTICE mode.

clinic-flows-reception-to-phlebotomy.name: ClinicOperationsPrototype

check-in flow, and Phlebotomy collection flow. The two feature datasets stay
deliberately independent because the upstream queue handoff is not yet a
proven executable contract.

clinic-flows-first-patient-journey.name: JourneyWorkspace
clinic-flows-first-patient-journey.description:
The journey conductor. It owns the episode and decides what comes next; the
phase surfaces below it only read their input and hand back their output.

The strip at the top is the point of the whole thing: at any moment it
states which step the episode is in, who is expected to act, and — when the
episode has stopped — what is holding it up. Money, specimen and result are
reported separately, because a paid visit with no sample drawn is a real
and common state.
